speaker: Narrator
text: It was a fine evening on this particular day in Tyresta National Park. The lake's temperature had increased by 2 degrees since noon, making it 16 degrees celsius. That's 60 degrees for you stupid yanks.

speaker: Narrator
text: An unfortunate infestation has been plaguing our aquatic friends in the lake, the fish. These creatures that sustain us are assaulted by parasites on all sides.

speaker: Narrator
text:
You may know Parasites as the vampire creatures that suck blood, like ticks or mosquitos.
	
	We humans like to consider anything that harms others for their own gain to be a parasite, though even we the human race, are ultimately parasites.

speaker: Narrator
text:
Christina was a young woman had a love of fish and their parasites. Her interest in the subject had barely been enough to get her through several years of grinding university work. 
	
	She had been antisocial, sometimes abrasive student in her upper secondary school years which scared off a bunch of classmates from being friends with her.
	
	The only exception is her classmate, Blanche, who doesn't seem to as afraid by her intimadating aura for some odd reason.

speaker: Narrator
text:
Christina always wore earbuds when she was out. To ensure that no one, absolutely no one, had an inkling of an idea that she should be approached. 
	
	Even now, Blanche can tell that she's been up for hours already at 8 in the morning, and she can smell that if she had showered in the intervening hours, she hadn't done it well enough.

speaker: Blanche
text: I'll take the rods and the net.

speaker: Blanche
text: Could you handle the... fishcooler and the bait?

speaker: Christina
text: Livewell, fish cooler, whatever...

speaker: Christina
text: I'll take it, yeah.

speaker: Narrator
text:
Christina sucks her teeth and puts her phone back into her pocket. She's been watching her favorite v-tuber on the way to the park. 
	
	The one benefit of this project was having an excuse to get up at ungodly hours of the morning when she could just about catch the end of their livestreams.

speaker: Narrator
text:
Tyresta National Park wasn’t the ideal place to do our research. They're both science undergrads enrolled at Stockholm University, where they are paired together for a project in a summer course. 
	
	Unlike Christina, Blanche had truly blossomed during her college years.

speaker: Narrator
text: Christina was always surprised of the disappointments of adulthood. That you'd wish to be free of the university playground but still have to fend for themselves as a full-blown working taxpayer.

speaker: Blanche
text: Christina.

speaker: Blanche
text: You seem tired.

speaker: Christina
text: What?

speaker: Blanche
text: Is that livewell too much for you to carry?

speaker: Christina
text: I'm just taking my time, it's nice out!

speaker: Narrator
text: They reach the spot and set up the fish cooler and the maggot-filled bait box, taking across the river opening to the sea, and wait for the little fish to bite.

speaker: Blanche
text: Once again, you don’t need to handle the fish like that. I hope I'm not insisting too much.

speaker: Christina
text: What kind of research can be done if we don't periodically inspect the fish?

speaker: Christina
text: It's a bad enough idea to put the sick and the healthy together in the same water when-

speaker: Blanche
text: Oh, a bite!

speaker: Narrator
text:
They needed to study something that would be exposed to pathogens in both salt and fresh water, didn't have value to industry, and, above all else, something Christina found to be a little cute. 
	
	The common roach. A little fish that hides among the underwater plants in fresh, brackish, or ocean waters, unable to decide whether it prefers the sea or the river. 
	
	It is tasteless to everyone but the Finns.

speaker: Blanche
text: You of all people should know, we have plenty of examples already living at the aquarium to measure against.

speaker: Christina
text: A pampered creature with no idea of the struggle of living in nature can't be used as a real control in a proper study.

speaker: Blanche
text: Oh, oh, I guess so!

speaker: Narrator
text:
First fish, healthy on the outside, quite clean as if it knew of hygiene, as much as a fish could. Blanche has stepped into the water herself. 
	
	The two of them catch fish after fish. About half of them are as perfect a specimen of roach as could be: shiny, healthy, happy. The other half...

speaker: Blanche
text: These... uh look creepy.

speaker: Christina
text: Creepy? Are you afraid of fish now?

speaker: Blanche
text: Some of these fish have been infested with a parasite in their eyes, haven't you noticed?

speaker: Christina
text: "What looks like rapid eye movements in the pupil seems to be the work of another creature that's living within the fishs' eyes."

speaker: Christina
text: Something I wrote down about 15 minutes ago, what took you so long?

speaker: Blanche
text: Let's catch more fish.

speaker: Narrator
text: Another round of fish was caught. Some of the catch in the livewell have regained their composure, darting around in the water.

speaker: Christina
text: I've got an idea, but it might be bad...

speaker: Blanche
text: Oh, Miss Christina Nobel has an idea, please enlighten me!

speaker: Christina
text: Hold on, I can't hear you over my Pasa- I mean my podcast.

speaker: Blanche
text: [i]Oh, a podcast?[/i]

speaker: Narrator
text: Blanche scoffs as she murmurs to herself in playful annoyance. Christina shrugs and continues with her thought.

speaker: Christina
text: I'm throwing back the some of healthy ones and leaving the sick. It'll reduce the sample size for the control group but I don't think we have enough room for all of them.

speaker: Blanche
text: So we'll do what I wanted us to do from the very start?

speaker: Christina
text: Sure, sure.

speaker: Christina
text: I'll take care of separating the ones we have now.

speaker: Blanche
text: With your bare hands?

speaker: Christina
text: Shouldn’t be an issue.

speaker: Narrator
text:
Chrisina separates the fish. Carefully observing the eye and other areas for signs of disease and freeing the healthy ones. 
	
	Her hand moves under the water to grasp the very last roach to be examined and sorted. Then, something quite odd happens.
	
	With her hand below the water, she felt something. It was not just the warmth of the water, not just the fish, something else.

speaker: Christina
text: AGH!

speaker: Narrator
text: Christina drops the roach between her fingers.

speaker: Blanche
text: What happened? Are you okay?

speaker: Christina
text: I think so.

speaker: Narrator
text:
She flashes her hand at Blanche, which seems untouched. Her knuckles are facing towards Blance which gave her the urge to jokingly flip off Blanche, but she decided not to. 
	
	She looks back to the fish she just dropped and examined it. It was healthy although limp now. Everything looks fine.

speaker: Christina
text: Healthy, baby smooth too.

speaker: Christina
text: It must've thrashed against my hand when I grabbed it.

speaker: Blanche
text: Hmm.

speaker: Blanche
text: We don't need that many more fish; we’ll just wrap up earlier than we expected today.

speaker: Christina
text: Good, I have other things to attend to.

speaker: Blanche
text: A job?

speaker: Christina
text: It's more important than that.

speaker: Christina
text: Uh huh.

speaker: Narrator
text:
Christina winces from pain. On the tip of her finger, she can feel something, writhing inside. The slight saltiness of the water surrounding her fingertip stung badly. 
	
	She thinks to herself that she can cope with the pain if she had some alone time to herself. Pasaya could make her feel better, she's probably live right now.

speaker: Narrator
text:
They finished gathering the fish. About 40% were relatively normal, and 60% were Infected. 
	
	Christina can still feel it. A wave of heat pulsates through her veins, brushing up against the walls of her bloodstream clumsily. 
	
	It was as if a child was inside her finger running their hand down the wooden walls of an old house. She also notices it’s getting harder to breathe.

speaker: Christina
text: Hahh, hahh.

speaker: Christina
text: Good... work...

speaker: Narrator
text:
Moving the livewell was harder now that it was filled with fish and water. Christina can feel something wrong as she takes one omninous step towards the car park. 
	
	Blanche sees her sweating. It was hard to tell what was drenched by the river and what was drowned out by perspiration.

speaker: Blanche
text: You were lively earlier today, but now I can see maggots getting to you in a week.

speaker: Christina
text: I'm fine.
